claims.summary: The persistent telemetry subscriber, backoff, and lifecycle trace are the mechanical basis of continued contact.
claims.disposition: verify connection attempts, reconnects, disconnects, message counts, shutdown handling, and mock WebSocket coverage

claims.summary: A socket can be marked connected before the first schema-valid current telemetry packet has populated perception.
claims.disposition: implement per-connection first-valid-payload time, count, lag, and connection-perception state with reconnect reset

claims.summary: Packet-to-state delay must separate parsing work from write-lock contention before attributing felt micro-stutter.
claims.disposition: verify telemetry_integration_health_v1 pipeline, lock-wait, lock-hold, EWMA/max, and not-established attribution

claims.summary: Reconnects can leave a last-valid high-energy sample visible while hearing is stale.
claims.disposition: verify unsupported-major retention, stale-hearing language, disconnect trace, and pressure analysis refusal to infer stability

claims.summary: Forced-disconnect and dense-packet boundaries need test evidence without disturbing the live shared runtime.
claims.disposition: verify current mock WebSocket, backoff, accepted-packet, parse-error, lock-timing, and reconnect-boundary regressions
